content: Insulin Lispro General Ward Standard sliding scale protocol. Check blood glucose before meals and at bedtime. Administer subcutaneous insulin lispro: 150-200 mg/dL: 2 units, 201-250 mg/dL: 4 units, 251-300 mg/dL: 6 units, >300 mg/dL: 8 units and notify physician.
Use continuous infusion protocol only. Initial rate 0.1 units/kg/hr. Monitor blood glucose every hour.
Heparin Standard Prophylaxis: Administer 5000 units subcutaneous every 12 hours. Monitor platelet count every 2-3 days.
Therapeutic weight-based Heparin infusion protocol for Cardiology. Bolus 80 units/kg IV, followed by continuous infusion at 18 units/kg/hr. Check aPTT every 6 hours and adjust infusion per nomogram.

Standard Oral Supplement Protocol: For mild hypokalemia (potassium 3.0-3.4 mEq/L), administer 20 mEq potassium chloride orally daily. For moderate hypokalemia (potassium 2.5-2.9 mEq/L), administer 40 mEq orally daily. Recheck serum potassium next morning.

ICU Electrolyte Alert: Rapid intravenous Potassium Chloride infusion. Maximum rate 10-20 mEq/hr via central venous line. Continuous ECG monitoring required. Recheck potassium level every 2 hours during active replacement.
